questions.statement: Under which article can the High Courts in India issue a writ (order)?
Which of the following maintains the turgidity of the cell?
Raut nacha is performed by mostly tribals of a state of India. Which of the following states is it?
Which among the following a cold&nbsp;current ?
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is 'Canarie'

Key Points

  • Canarie Current:
    • The Canarie Current is a cold oceanic current flowing along the northwest coast of Africa, from the Iberian Peninsula towards the equator.
    • It is part of the North Atlantic Gyre, a large system of circular ocean currents driven by global wind patterns and Earth's rotation.
    • The Canarie Current brings cooler water from higher latitudes towards the tropics, significantly influencing the climate of coastal areas in Northwest Africa.
    • Its cooling effect contributes to arid conditions in regions like the Sahara Desert, as it reduces evaporation and rainfall along the coast.
    • This current plays a role in nutrient cycling, supporting marine ecosystems and fisheries in the region by bringing nutrient-rich waters to the surface.

Additional Information

  • Agulhas Current:
    • The Agulhas Current is a warm ocean current flowing along the east coast of South Africa, moving southwest into the Indian Ocean.
    • It is characterized by high temperatures and salinity, contributing to the warm climate in the coastal regions it passes.
    • Unlike the Canarie Current, the Agulhas Current does not have a cooling effect; instead, it enhances evaporation and rainfall in its vicinity.
  • Brazil Current:
    • The Brazil Current is a warm ocean current flowing along the east coast of South America, from the equator to the southern tip of Brazil.
    • It is part of the South Atlantic Gyre and carries warm tropical waters southward.
    • It influences the coastal climate by increasing temperatures and humidity, contrasting sharply with the cooling effects of the Canarie Current.
  • Gulf Stream:
    • The Gulf Stream is a warm ocean current originating in the Gulf of Mexico and flowing across the Atlantic Ocean towards Europe.
    • It significantly impacts the climate of the eastern United States and Western Europe, bringing warmth to these regions.
    • The Gulf Stream is one of the fastest and most powerful ocean currents but is fundamentally different from cold currents like the Canarie Current.

Important Points

  • Cold currents like the Canarie Current are typically found in higher latitudes and flow towards the equator, helping to regulate global temperatures.
  • Warm currents, such as the Gulf Stream, Brazil Current, and Agulhas Current, originate in tropical or equatorial regions and move towards higher latitudes, warming the areas they pass.
  • The interaction between cold and warm currents plays a crucial role in shaping global weather patterns, marine ecosystems, and climate zones.
  • Understanding ocean currents is vital for studying climate change, maritime navigation, and the distribution of marine biodiversity.
